name: Zerodha LL Two-Pointer Toolkit
scenario: Build fast/slow pointer utilities for Zerodha's order processing system.

core.description: hasCycle(head) + detectCycleStart(head): Floyd's O(n) O(1). Test: cycle→entry node, no cycle→null.
isPalindrome(head): find middle + reverse second half + compare. O(n) O(1). Test: [1,2,2,1]→true, [1,2]→false.
getIntersection(headA,headB): two-pointer redirect. O(n+m) O(1). Test: shared node value correct.
@SpringBootTest (4 tests): middle correct, cycle entry correct, palindrome true/false, intersection correct.
README: explains why Floyd's Phase 2 (reset slow to head) finds cycle entry.

bonus.description: nthFromEnd(head,n): fast/slow gap n. O(n) O(1).
reorderList(head): L0→Ln→L1→Ln-1... Find middle, reverse second half, interleave.

ai_policy_explanation: 5 hints. findMiddle: fast=head.next so slow stops at END of first half for even-length lists. fast=head would stop slow at first of two middles. hasCycle: while(fast!=null&&fast.next!=null): slow=slow.next, fast=fast.next.next. If slow==fast: cycle. detectCycleStart: after phase1, reset slow=head. Both advance at speed 1. Meet at cycle entry. isPalindrome: (1) find middle with fast=head, (2) reverse slow.next onwards, (3) compare head vs reversed head until reversed is null. getIntersection: a=a==null?headB:a.next; b=b==null?headA:b.next; Both travel total n+m steps → meet at intersection or both null.
